basics.name: Hanze Guo
basics.summary: Ph.D. student at the Gaoling School of Artificial Intelligence, Renmin University of China. Research interests include LLM alignment, personalized reasoning, information retrieval, and recommendation systems.

work.name: Tencent
work.position: Qingyun Program Intern, Yuanbao Agent Algorithm Group
highlights: Work on multimodal search agents for VLM search scenarios.
Explore visual-language query understanding, visual information parsing, multimodal evidence retrieval, and answer generation.
Contribute to evaluation, data construction, and strategy optimization for real-world multimodal search pipelines.

work.name: Kuaishou
work.position: Intern, Search Technology Department, Behavior Large Model Group
highlights: Deployed Qwen-scale 100B models and conducted downstream reasoning distillation experiments.
Explored how to introduce LLM reasoning into personalized search tasks.
Trained LLM reasoning capabilities with full-parameter fine-tuning, CoT-based SFT, and GRPO on real business data.

work.name: Microsoft Research Asia
highlights: Worked on aligning large language models with pluralistic human preferences.
Developed counterfactual reasoning methods for controllable fine-grained preference adjustment.
Improved interpretability while maintaining personalized alignment performance.

work.name: OPPO Research Institute
highlights: Studied how to adapt LLMs to general time-series forecasting tasks.
Investigated the roles of text prompts and time-series signals in forecasting.
Explored modeling, alignment, and fusion strategies for text-time-series information.

education.institution: University of Electronic Science and Technology of China

publications.name: Counterfactual Reasoning for Steerable Pluralistic Value Alignment of Large Language Models
publications.summary: A counterfactual reasoning framework for steerable pluralistic value alignment of large language models.

publications.name: SoREX: Towards Self-Explainable Social Recommendation with Relevant Ego-Path Extraction
publications.publisher: ACM Transactions on Information Systems, 2025, CCF-A
publications.summary: A self-explainable social recommendation framework with relevant ego-path extraction.

publications.name: Why not Collaborative Filtering in Dual View? Bridging Sparse and Dense Models
publications.publisher: ACM Transactions on Information Systems, 2026, CCF-A
publications.summary: A plug-and-play dual-view recommendation framework that bridges sparse interaction structures and dense model representations.
